data.title: Loma Negra reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Loma Negra press release (LOMA): Q4

  • Net sales revenues stood at Ps. 225,233 million (US$ 152 million), and decreased by 1.7% YoY, mainly explained by a decrease of 4.4% in in the top line of the Cement segment.

  • Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA reached Ps. 44,293 million, decreasing by 33.4% YoY in pesos, while in dollars it reached 37 million, down 26.9% from 4Q24.

  • The Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA margin stood at 19.7%, decreasing by 938 basis points YoY from 29.0%.

  • Net Profit of Ps. 5,895 million, compared to a net profit of Ps. 29,096 million in the same period of the previous year, mainly driven by a lower operational performance, coupled with a loss in net financial results.

  • Net Debt stood at Ps. 266,492 million (US$183 million), representing a Net Debt/LTM Adjusted EBITDA ratio of 1.47x, compared to 0.89x in FY24.

data.title: Evogene outlines ChemPass AI-driven strategy and anticipates new collaborations amid organizational realignment
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Evogene Ltd. (EVGN) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ofer Haviv emphasized a strategic transformation, stating Evogene "made deliberate choice to sharpen our focus and execution" by concentrating resources on designing small molecules for drug development and agricultural chemicals via the ChemPass AI engine.
  • Haviv explained, "we focused our technology development on a single computational engine, ChemPass AI... to concentrate exclusively on 2 high-impact markets where ChemPass AI offers strong differentiation, human health centered on small molecule drugs and agriculture focused on novel ag chemicals."
  • He highlighted proprietary advancements, including a partnership with Google Cloud: "We tripled our benchmarks for accuracy, delivering 90% design precision," and announced a new collaboration to integrate advanced AI agents for enhanced automation and workflow.
  • In human health, Haviv reported multiple partnered drug discovery programs and four disclosed collaborations, with scaling expected. In agriculture, the AgPlenus subsidiary continues collaborations with Bayer and Corteva, and an internal pipeline of novel herbicides and fungicides.
  • CFO Yaron Eldad stated, "operating expenses net... declined to approximately $13.8 million for the year ended 2025 compared to approximately $22 million in 2024." He added, "total operating expenses net of approximately $3.2 million compared to approximately $4.3 million in the corresponding period of 2024."
  • Eldad reported the sale of Lavie Bio operations to ICL, the winding down of Biomica, and the integration of AgPlenus into core operations. He also noted the exercise of Series A and B warrants for $3.4 million in gross proceeds.

Outlook

  • Management expects the reduced expense level to be sustained, and anticipates cash from Lavie Bio and Biomica will "satisfy our need for at least mid next year, maybe even more." Haviv indicated, "even without additional financial transaction, we have sufficient cash for a little bit more than 1.5 years."
  • Haviv outlined three key catalysts in the next 12 months: additional technology collaborations (such as with Google), new collaboration agreements with pharma and biotech companies, and partnership agreements with chemical companies. He noted, "each time that such a thing happen, it really pushed the limitation that we are addressing with our technology to further and further."

Financial Results

  • Revenues for 2025 were $3.9 million, down from $5.6 million in 2024, mainly due to lower AgPlenus activity and completed collaboration agreements.
  • Fourth quarter 2025 revenues were $0.3 million, compared to $1.5 million the previous year, largely due to reduced seed sales by Casterra.
  • Cost of revenues rose to $4.1 million for 2025 from $2.4 million in 2024, driven by a $2.2 million inventory impairment at Casterra.
  • R&D expenses net of grants were $8 million for 2025, down from $12.5 million in 2024, attributed to reductions in Biomica, Casterra, and AgPlenus.
  • Operating loss for 2025 was $14 million, a decrease from $18.8 million in 2024, while net loss for 2025 was $7.8 million versus $18.1 million in 2024, reflecting cost reductions and income from discontinued operations.
  • As of December 31, 2025, cash, cash equivalents, and short-term deposits totaled $13 million.

Q&A

  • Analyst asked about the terms of the BMC128 license agreement with Lishan Pharmaceuticals. Haviv responded the agreement includes milestone payments and revenue sharing based on commercial transactions, stating, "the numbers will be quite significant... it could be significant for Evogene."
  • On potential cash inflows from Lavie Bio and Biomica, and catalysts over 12 months, Haviv stated the cash distribution would "satisfy our need for at least mid next year," and outlined the three types of catalysts: technology collaborations, pharma/biotech collaborations, and chemical company partnerships. Haviv emphasized, "it's very important for me to make sure that it's very clear that what is the strategic avenue Evogene decided to go through."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were focused on cash runway, future catalysts, and partnership monetization, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone, with questions centering on specifics of agreements and cash flow.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, repeatedly highlighting the company's streamlined focus, cash outlook, and strategic partnerships. In Q&A, Haviv was direct and detailed, stating, "we truly believe this represent the highest potential for our shareholders for the next few years."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's confidence appeared consistent, while analysts' focus continued to be on tangible near-term milestones and financial durability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter showed further implementation of the strategic focus on ChemPass AI and divestment of non-core assets. Management language shifted from an evolving strategy to a more finalized, focused direction.
  • Financial discipline was reinforced, with continued reductions in operating, R&D, and marketing expenses. The sale or wind-down of non-core subsidiaries progressed, with cash position and expense control highlighted as strengths.
  • Analysts' key concerns remained consistent: monetization of technology, cash runway, and partnership progress. Management's tone remained confident, with more emphasis on imminent partnerships and the sustainability of the business model.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited regional geopolitical risks, referencing the aftermath of the war in Israel and broader Middle East destabilization as potential challenges.
  • CFO Eldad noted the risk of falling revenues, particularly from AgPlenus and Casterra, and flagged the inventory impairment as a significant one-time cost.
  • The company is mitigating risks through cost reductions, narrowing strategic focus, and seeking new partnership revenues.

Final Takeaway

Evogene’s management highlighted a strategic transformation centered on ChemPass AI, with organizational streamlining and divestment of non-core assets supporting a clear path toward value creation. Collaborations with technology leaders, a focus on human health and ag chemicals, and a strengthened cash position are emphasized as key enablers for new partnerships and sustainable growth. Management outlined multiple potential catalysts for the coming year and reinforced confidence in the company’s ability to deliver innovation and financial stability through disciplined execution of its refined strategy.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: CBL buys mall in Nebraska, sells open-air center
data.description:
CBL Properties (CBL) acquired an enclosed shopping center in Lincoln, Nebraska, for $43.5M and agreed to sell an open-air shopping center in two separate transactions, furthering its strategy of pursuing high-yield enclosed mall properties, the company said on Thursday.

The retail REIT acquired Gateway Mall from Washington Prime Group (WPG) and financed the deal with a $21.0M non-recourse, five-year loan that carries a fixed interest rate of 6.46%.

The contract for the sale of the open-air center is at an ~8% capitalization rate. The transaction, expected to generate net proceeds after debt repayment of $25M, is anticipated to close in April. 

"This acquisition was accomplished at attractive pricing and aligns with our strategy to pursue high-yield enclosed mall opportunities where our operating expertise and capital discipline can drive long‑term value," said CBL (CBL) CEO Stephen D. Lebovitz. "In addition, the pending sale of another open-air center at an attractive cap rate demonstrates our ongoing progress in recycling capital from stabilized assets into new investments that are accretive to our cash flow yield and support our long‑term growth."

CBL (CBL) stock edged up 0.2% in after-hours trading.

data.title: REalloys announces proposed public offering of common stock
data.description:
• REalloys (ALOY) on Thursday said it intends to offer and sell shares of its common stock in an underwritten public offering.
  • In addition, the company intends to grant the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase additional shares of common stock.
  • All of the shares of common stock in the offering are to be offered by the company. 
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds of the proposed offering for working capital and for general corporate purposes.
  • ALOY -8.11% after hours to $19.93.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Aecon Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.52, revenue of C$1.54B beats by $510M
data.description:
• Aecon press release (AEGXF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.52.
  • Revenue of C$1.54B (+21.3% Y/Y) beats by C$510M.

data.title: GEO Group announces CFO transition
data.description:
• The GEO Group (GEO) announced that chief financial officer Mark Suchinski will leave the company effective March 31, 2026.
  • Shayn March, currently executive vice president, Finance and Treasurer, will become chief financial officer effective April 1, 2026.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Costco adjusted comparable sales grew 6.7%
Methode Electronics to sell dataMate copper transceiver business to Bel Fuse
data.description:
• Methode Electronics (MEI) on Thursday said it will sell its dataMate copper transceiver business to Bel Fuse to sharpen its focus on growth strategies in power solutions.
  • The company said the divestiture will allow it to concentrate on high-density power delivery products, particularly for data center applications and power distribution aimed at increasingly dense AI computing architectures.
  • Methode added that the deal is part of its strategy to refine its portfolio, reduce debt and streamline its facilities footprint.
  • The dataMate unit supplies transceivers to enterprise networking equipment manufacturers and telecom network operators and develops data-over-copper solutions, including single pair powered ethernet for industrial applications.
  • BELFA -0.0% after hours to $184.45.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Costco sees stronger sales growth than retail peers in FQ2
data.description:
Costco Wholesale Corporation (COST) dipped slightly in postmarket trading on Thursday after disclosing its fiscal second-quarter earnings report. Total sales were up 9.2% to $69.6 billion in FQ2, which was higher growth than other major retailers such as Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), Best Buy (BBY), and Dollar General (DG) saw with their reports that overlapped the winter holidays.

Comparable sales were up 7.4% during the quarter that ended on February 15 to beat the consensus estimate of 6.7%. The big box retailer said comparable sales were 5.9% higher in the U.S. vs. 5.7% consensus, rose 10.1% in Canada, and were 13.0% higher in international markets. The Chinese New Year occurred on February 17, 19 days later this year. The shift positively impacted February international sales by approximately 4.0%. E-commerce sales rose 22.6% during the quarter. 

After backing out gas prices and F/X swings, comparable sales were up 6.4% in the U.S. and were 6.7% higher overall. 

Membership fee income was $1.33B vs. $1.17B a year ago and $1.33B consensus. Net income for the quarter was $2.04B, vs. $1.79B a year ago.

At the end of the quarter, Costco (COST) operated 924 warehouses, including 634 in the United States and Puerto Rico, 114 in Canada, 42 in Mexico, 37 in Japan, 29 in the United Kingdom, 20 in Korea, 15 in Australia, 14 in Taiwan, seven in China, five in Spain, three in France, two in Sweden, and one each in Iceland and New Zealand. Costco also operates e-commerce sites in the U.S., Canada, the U.K., Mexico, Korea, Taiwan, Japan, and Australia.

Costco (COST) was up 0.2% in postmarket action to $984.53 vs. a 52-week trading range of $844.06 to $1,067.08.

data.title: The Cooper Companies Non-GAAP EPS of $1.10 beats by $0.07, revenue of $1.02B in-line
data.description:
• The Cooper Companies press release (COO): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.10 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $1.02B (+5.7% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Gross margin of 68% similar to last year’s first quarter. On a non-GAAP basis, gross margin was down 60 basis points from last year to 68%. Excluding the impact of tariffs, gross margin would have been flat year over year.
  • Operating margin of 21% compared with 19% in last year’s first quarter driven by operating expense leverage. On a non-GAAP basis, operating margin was up 180 basis points from last year to 27%, reflecting disciplined execution and meaningful synergies delivered through last year's reorganization, and leverage from last year’s IT implementations. 
  • Interest expense of $22.4 million compared with $26.0 million in last year's first quarter driven by lower average debt.
  • Cash provided by operations of $260.9 million, offset by capital expenditures of $102.2 million resulted in free cash flow of $158.7 million.
